title: Low power data generation for iris-related detection and authentication
abstract: Sensing of scene-based occurrences is disclosed. In one example, a vision sensor system comprises (1) dedicated computer vision (CV) computation hardware configured to receive sensor data from at least one sensor array and capable of computing CV features using readings from multiple neighboring sensor pixels and (2) a first processing unit communicatively coupled with the dedicated CV computation hardware. The vision sensor system is configured to, in response to processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating a presence of one or more irises in a scene captured by the at least one sensor array, generate data in support of iris-related operations to be performed by a second processing unit and send the generated data to the second processing unit.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A vision sensor system for sensing scene-based occurrences, the vision sensor system comprising: dedicated computer vision (CV) computation hardware configured to receive sensor data from at least one sensor array comprising a plurality of sensor pixels and capable of computing one or more CV features using readings from multiple neighboring sensor pixels within the at least one sensor array; and a first processing unit communicatively coupled with the dedicated CV computation hardware, wherein the vision sensor system is configured to, in response to processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating a presence of one or more irises in a scene captured by the at least one sensor array, generate data in support of iris-related operations to be performed in a second processing unit and send the generated data to the second processing unit. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating the presence of the one or more irises in the scene comprises the one or more computed CV features indicating a presence of at least one face in the scene. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the generated data is indicative of location of one or more eyes. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the generated data is indicative of a size for each of one or more eyes. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the generated data comprises location data for a plurality of landmarks associated with one or more eyes. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the plurality of landmarks comprise four landmarks, including a first landmark and a second landmark corresponding to a left corner and a right corner, respectively, of a first eye and a third landmark and a 

 fourth landmark corresponding to a left corner and a right corner, respectively, of a second eye. 7. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the vision sensor system is configured to generate the location data for the plurality of landmarks using a trained classifier. 8. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the trained classifier comprises a plurality of cascaded stages. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least one sensor array comprises two sensor arrays, a visual sensor array configured to capture visible light and an infrared (IR) sensor array configured to capture IR light, wherein the vision sensor system is further configured to send an indication for an IR light source to be lit, in response to the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating the presence of one or more irises in the scene, and wherein the vision sensor system is configured to generate the data in support of iris-related operations using readings from the IR sensor array captured while the IR light source is lit. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the visual sensor array is configured to capture images at a first resolution, and the IR sensor array is configured to capture images at a second resolution higher than the first resolution. 11. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least one sensor array comprises an IR-capable visual sensor array capable of capturing both visual light and IR light, wherein the vision sensor system is further configured to send an indication for an IR light source to be lit, in response to the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating the presence of one or more irises in the scene, and 

 wherein the vision sensor system is configured to generate the data in support of iris-related operations using readings from the IR-capable visual sensor array captured while the IR light source is lit. 12. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the vision sensor system is further configured to generate an event in response to the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating the presence of the one or more irises in the scene captured by the at least one sensor array, and send the event to the second processing unit; and wherein the second processing unit is configured to, in response to receiving the event, transition from a first power state to a second power state, the second power state being associated with a higher power level than the first power state. 13. A method of sensing scene-based occurrences comprising: receiving, by dedicated computer vision (CV) computation hardware in a vision sensor system comprising the dedicated CV computation hardware and a first processing unit communicatively coupled with the dedicated CV computation hardware, sensor data from at least one sensor array comprising a plurality of sensor pixels; computing, by the dedicated CV computation hardware, one or more CV features using readings from multiple neighboring sensor pixels within the at least one sensor array; generating, by the vision sensor system, in response to processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating a presence of one or more irises in a scene captured by the at least one sensor array, data in support of iris-related operations to be performed in a second processing unit; and sending the generated data to the second processing unit. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating the presence of the one or more irises in the scene captured by the at least one sensor array comprises the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating a presence of at least one face in the scene. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the generated data is indicative of location of one or more eyes. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the generated data is indicative of a size for each of one or more eyes. 17. The method of claim 13, wherein the generated data comprises location data for a plurality of landmarks associated with to one or more eyes. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the plurality of landmarks comprise four landmarks, including a first landmark and a second landmark corresponding to a left corner and a right corner, respectively, of a first eye and a third landmark and a fourth landmark corresponding to a left corner and a right corner, respectively, of a second eye. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the vision sensor system is configured to generate the location data for the plurality of landmarks using a trained classifier. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the trained classifier comprises a plurality of cascaded stages. 21. The method of claim 13, further comprising sending, by the vision sensor system, an indication for an infrared (IR) light source to be lit, in response to the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating the presence of the one or more irises in the scene captured by the at least one sensor array, wherein the generating, by the vision sensor system, the data in support of iris- related operations comprises generating the data using readings from an IR sensor array captured while the IR light source is lit, and wherein the at least one sensor array comprises two sensor arrays, a visual sensor array configured to capture visible light and the IR sensor array configured to capture IR light. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the visual sensor array is configured to capture images at a first resolution, and the IR sensor array is configured to capture images at a second resolution higher than the first resolution. 23. The method of claim 13, further comprising sending, by the vision sensor system, an indication for an IR light source to be lit, in response to the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating the presence of the one or more irises in the scene captured by the at least one sensor array, wherein the generating, by the vision sensor system, the data in support of iris- related operations comprises generating the data using readings from the an IR-capable visual sensor array captured while the IR light source is lit, and wherein the at least one sensor array comprises the IR-capable visual sensor array, the IR-capable visual sensor capable of capturing both visual light and IR light. 24. The method of claim 13, further comprising generating, by the vision sensor system, an event in response to the processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating the presence of the one or more irises in the scene captured by the at least one sensor array; and sending, by the vision sensor system, the event to the second processing unit, wherein the second processing unit is configured to, in response to receiving the event, transition from a first power state to a second power state, the second power state being associated with a higher power level than the first power state. 25. A system of sensing scene-based occurrences comprising: means for receiving sensor data from at least one sensor array comprising a plurality of sensor pixels; hardware means for computing one or more CV features using readings from multiple neighboring sensor pixels within the at least one sensor array; means for processing the one or more CV features to indicate a presence of one or more irises in a scene captured by the at least one sensor array; 

 means for generating, in response to an indication of the presence of the one or more irises in the scene captured by the at least one sensor array, data in support of iris- related operations to be performed in a second processing unit; and means for sending the generated data to the second processing unit. 26. The system of claim 25, wherein the means for generating in support of iris-related operations comprises means for generating location data corresponding to a plurality of landmarks defined with respect to one or more eyes. 27. The system of claim 26, wherein the means for generating corresponding to the plurality of landmarks defined with respect to one or more eyes comprises a trained classifier. 28. A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing instructions therein for execution by one or more processing units, comprising instructions to: control receipt, by dedicated computer vision (CV) computation hardware in a vision sensor system comprising the dedicated CV computation hardware and a first processing unit communicatively coupled to the dedicated CV computation hardware, sensor data from at least one sensor array comprising a plurality of sensor pixels; control computation, by the dedicated CV computation hardware, one or more CV features using readings from multiple neighboring sensor pixels within the at least one sensor array; control generation, by the vision sensor system, in response to processing of the one or more computed CV features indicating a presence of one or more irises in a scene captured by the at least one sensor array, data in support of iris-related operations to be performed in a second processing unit; and control sending of the generated data to the second processing unit. 29. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 28, wherein the generated data comprises location data for a plurality of landmarks associated with one or more eyes. 30. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 29, wherein the location data for the plurality of landmarks is generated using a trained classifier.
